items.title: I Can Do Bad All By Myself
items.summary: Today, I want to talk about better bad decisions. Now, I'm…
items.content_html:
Today, I want to talk about better bad decisions.

Now, I'm not a health guru, but I play one on the internet.

No, you don't.

This one is for your own good.

Another one of my (few) rules.

If you're going to make a bad decision, make a better one.

A better bad decision.

For example.

You're craving a burger.

Juicy.

Fatty.

Plump.

🍔

So you don't go to the terrible spots.

You go to Steak 'n Shake.

They do use beef tallow.

And since you're here, you're thirsty.

So you get a Coke Zero instead of regular.

Zero calories.

But a ton of (acquired) taste.

Better, bad, decision.

You got me?

We still ordering large fries because my goodness, we're Americans.

🍟

Just don't eat them all.

I used to never order fries, making it a best bad decision. 

But I digest digress.

Split the meal into two meals.

Half burger, half fries, half drink.

Then you got another meal in two hours.

Better, bad, decisions.

I'm convinced that it's the "AND THEN"s that get folks into trouble.

Too many baddies stacked on top of one another.

Eating a bunch of 'em.

Your attitude should never be, "Might as well..."

It should be, "Well, I got my main bad, that's enough for me."

Ideally, no side bads.

You can do bad all by yourself.

Just use my rule.

Trust me.

But you say not to trust you all the time.

People actually read this sheet?

items.title: Keep On
items.summary: When is the happiest you have ever been? As a stoic…
items.content_html:
When is the happiest you have ever been?

As a stoic curmudgeon, this is an interesting question to me.

And so I ask it of you.

When were you the happiest?

Brightest eyed?

Bushiest-taileded?

He made it an -est word for emphasis and added extra -ed at the end of tailed for (hopeful) comedic effect.

Is it now?

Or in childhood?

Or perhaps, as it could be, it has yet to happen?

Still though, the happiest moment thus far.

What was it?

What is it?

A single moment?

A single season?

A single run?

I will tell you, if you can keep a secret, that all of my happiest moments have occurred after I fell out.

After my nuclear fall out.

☢

After I almost touched God.

It's funny how you realize how trivial most things are once things get serious.

Having a come to Jesus moment, but hoping you don't get to meet the man Himself.

Just yet.

Just yet.

This truck's got a lot of miles on it.

But it's a good truck.

A lot of life left in it.

I hope.

Is he referring to himself as an automobile?

I hope.

But, if it's not the case, I want to tell you something.

The happiest I have ever been is right now.

This era.

This phase.

This iteration.

Just jipper.

So thankful for what has come, what will come, and what is.

Amen.

😋

Keep on, my fellow vehicle.

🚚

items.title: Even Me
items.summary: Can we keep it lighthearted, today? Let's do that. Keep it…
items.content_html:
Can we keep it lighthearted, today?

Let's do that.

Keep it light.

Hearted.

Cheerful.

Okay!

Have you ever trusted anyone to the level that one of them lil' flipping cheerleaders do?

I mean the ones that flip, fly, and soar and blindly trust that someone will (try to) catch them in mid-air?

I don't know why I just thought of that.

But it does require a level of trust that just seems, oh what's the word, silly.

Silly.

An asymmetric risk the other way around.

Low reward.

Moderate to high risk.

Terrible bet.

Of course, my favorite wisdom is DTA.

Don't.

Trust.

Anybody.

You see me flip flop flying in the air, don't try to catch me.

Trust me.

Something has obviously gone horribly wrong.

Let me handle it.

Let me land.

But you promised to keep it lighthearted!

Oh right.

Like a mother motherin' comic book character.

Fist down.

Head down.

Crater the ground.

Head up.

[The entire audience erupts in applause.]

Don't.

[Clap clap.]

Trust.

[Clap clap.]

Anyyyyyyy....

Body!!!

Yay!!!

🥂

Go Humans!!!

Woooooooo!!!!

items.title: Tickling Ivory
items.summary: [You find the author strumming away at a grand piano. He…
items.content_html:
[You find the author strumming away at a grand piano. He seems surprised to see you.]

Well hello there.

How are you?

...

Oh, I'm fine.

I'm fine.

Thank you for asking.

Just working the keys here.

That's not a piano, that's a MacBook.

Comme ci, comme ca, mon amie.

🎹

items.title: One of One
items.summary: 🎵 "Come They told me." 🎵 You know something? You are…
items.content_html:
🎵 "Come

They told me."  🎵

You know something?

You are one in a million.

Truly.

There are only about 8,300 of you walking this blue marble.

What?

Does knowing there's a small town's worth of you running around make it less special?

Fine.

You're one in a billion!

Doing math. There's still sevenish others just like me??

One of eight!

Even at one in a billy, you're still not unique.

And we're not even counting other marbles.

There's still seven other versions of you walking around picking their nose being productive and reading musings columns.

One of the biggest fears of the supremacy of artificial intellect is that we'll lose that uniqueness of being human.

But I mean, aren't we already kinda there?

Humans are sort of the same, now.

Look around.

You see it.

Similar templates.

Repeated over and over.

Like the character generator got lazy and just started mass spawning mother mothers.

Who do you know that marches to the beat of their own 808?

To one of ones.

Thank you for being you.

🎵 "Peace on earth.

Can it be?" 🎵

Pa rum pum pum pum.

🥁

items.title: Weird To Think About
items.summary: Have you ever stopped to think. I'll get more specific here,…
items.content_html:
Have you ever stopped to think.

I'll get more specific here, but that's a fun point to pause on.

Ever stop?

To think?

Hmm.

💭

Have you ever stopped to think about how many generations of humans had to meet, get together, make some magic, and ya know.

Over and over.

How many women in your family tree had to risk their lives to give birth?

Just to, eventually, after a LOT OF LIFE RISKING, get to you?

[Looks you up and down, while holding a clipboard and writing furiously, as if evaluating your ancestors handiwork.]

Uhhh...

Walking around all able-bodied and reading musings columns on the internet?

Assuming you are able bodied.

Very sorry if you are not.

That's weird to think about right?

That's what I'm here for.

To be weird to think about.

Also, think about how many generations of humans are yet to be.

All beginning from the sparkle in your eye and the fruit of your looms.

And their looms.

And the looms before.

And after.

Looms all the way up.

And all the way down.

Just fruits sprouting everywhere.

Heirlooms.

And how, if anything went anyway different, you might not be here.

They might not be here.

Ain't that something?

To the human race.

And all the fun in making babies and raising 'em.

Pour one out for your 11th great grandparents and all the way down the line.

If they didn't find time off from whatever the heck they were doing to smack biscuits, you wouldn't be here.

Smack biscuits?

🤔

items.title: Iron Eagle
items.summary: Can we talk about the bald eagle? It seems like a…
items.content_html:
Can we talk about the bald eagle?

It seems like a good weekend to do so.

Folks.

It's time to rename the bird.

What?! Boooo!!!

Now hear me out.

Bald eagles are our nation's symbol.

But they ain't bald.

They got a magnificent dome of feathers.

Sure, they're white.

Contrasts with it's brown body.

But that don't make it bald.

It simply ain't a bald eagle.

Stone Cold Steve Eagle?

Exudes badarsery while honoring the bald thing?

No.

No, that's terrible.

Silver eagle?

Too old(ish) sounding.

Inferior to gold, too.

We want the name to exude apex strength.

But honor, too.

Honor, too.

Golden eagle?

I mean, that's more me.

I'm gold.

So, no.

Get your own adjective, bird.

AMEREAGLE!

[National anthem starts playing for some reason. Oh boy, it's the Whitney Super Bowl version. We pause the musing here while the anthem plays. The author standing and saluting the flag the entire time. When it's over, he wipes a single tear from his left eye and sits down again, marveling at how that lady could sing.]

No, not Amereagle.

Too gimmicky.

How about:

American Eagle!

No, no, that's a clothing brand.

Let me workshop this.

The bird needs a rebrand.

And that's a bald-faced truth.

This one's for you, Chappy.

Chappy eagle? 

He was bald.

Play the tape.

📻

USA! USA!! USA!!!

Remember, mother mothers.

[Jets fly overhead in missing man formation.]

🇺🇸

        🦅  🦅  🦅  🦅

items.title: Ho Yeah
items.summary: The most important part of a musings column is to muse.
items.content_html:
The most important part of a musings column is to muse.

I think that's right.

I want to muse you.

The crazy thing about me, amongst many things, is that if I'm tough on you, if I'm giving you a hard time, it means I care about you.

I give a sheet about you.

Because if I didn't, you'd be, like most are, invisible to me.

In.

Freakin.

Visible.

John Cenas.

Just not there.

This provides a wonderful backdrop for the people I love.

They get coach Al, sometimes.

Which is not always welcome.

Never.

But I hope to say, wherever you are and whoever you are, I see you.

I do.

Thank you.

And where the heck did you come from?

How long you been standing there?

That long?!?

Must have missed ya.

Stand up straight.

Like God's got a string on the top of your head and he's pulling up.

Unless you fighting.

Then you can curl up a little.

Put some bass in your voice.

Chin down.

Eyes up.

Don't bow to me, I ain't Miyagi.

When are you going to get your sheet together?

He warned you...

items.title: Drawing A Blank
items.summary: A lot of the time, when I was sickest, I'd ask…
items.content_html:
A lot of the time, when I was sickest, I'd ask questions.

To my team of awesome doctors.

And sometimes, more often than I thought they would, they would simply shrug.

I don't know.

Could be.

Yeah, that might be.

And I thought, well, if you don't know...

It occurred to me sometime down the road that I was sort of an experiment.

That what I was going through was rather unique.

Certainly to me, but also to the field at large.

A let's see what happens.

A "once in a career, if you're lucky" case one said, from their perspective.

I am lucky.

So while I was quite obviously anxious to know definites, there simply wasn't enough of a track record to really say.

Could I have really used something other than a "beats me" answer?

Sure.

Sure.

But I'm still here, ya'll.

Beats me how.

But I'll take it.

🤷‍♂️

I do know, though.

If you're asking.

Even if you're not.

I've been blessed my whole life.

Every glorious second.

Oddly, especially, when times are darkest.

🌚

items.title: Repeat For Emphasis
items.summary: Oh no, he got on his silver robes. I was going…
items.content_html:
Oh no, he got on his silver robes.

I was going to write about making holistic bets.

But I didn't, so I didn't.

This isn't a musings column about gambling, so I figured it might confuse people.

Stick to your brand!

I don't mean bets in a typical gambling sense.

More a life sense.

For example, how are you going to meet the love of your life, if you don't have the guts to even say hello?

And will you have the guts to leave them, if and when you know you picked wrong?

The largest wagers in your life aren't monetary.

The stakes are far higher.

And they are staggeringly asymmetric.

Staggeringly holistic.

Pick the right friends, vocation, loves and the world is your medium-rare steak.

Pick the wrong ones...

Wooh boy.

Something vegan.

Easy for me to say, you say.

Yes, I suppose it is.

Much harder to live it.

Do you live it?

And so, young(ish) hopper of grass, I leave you today with a few things.

First, a telepathic slap to the head.

Pay attention.

True currency is energy and time.

Spend both like a pincher of dimes.

Is reading this thing everyday a good use of either?

Ah who knows.

I just think of these things as I float through the universe.

Sitting indian-style (can we still say that?) and hovering in mid-air.

Catching flies with chopsticks like it ain't nothing.

Like it ain't nothing.

🧘‍♂️
